entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop used in intercropping experiments

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A legume crop used in intercropping experiments

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops in proximity to enhance productivity

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output and efficiency

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth

entities.name: BELOW-GROUND INTERACTIONS
entities.description: Interactions between plant roots and soil occurring beneath the surface

entities.name: POOR FERTILITY SOILS
entities.description: Soils characterized by a lack of essential nutrients

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region of the study, specifically semi-arid southern Africa

entities.name: ADDITIVE INTERCROPPING
entities.description: An experimental intercropping approach where crops are added to a system

entities.name: SOIL CHEMICAL VARIABLES
entities.description: Chemical properties of the soil measured during experiments

entities.name: ROOT DYNAMICS
entities.description: The growth, distribution, and behavior of plant roots

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of crop produced per unit area

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILISER
entities.description: The application of nitrogen-based nutrients to the soil

entities.name: AVAILABLE NITROGEN
entities.description: The amount of nitrogen in the soil that is accessible to plants

entities.name: MAIZE GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The specific weight of grains produced by maize

entities.name: OVER-YIELDING
entities.description: A condition where intercropped productivity exceeds that of sole crops

entities.name: LAND EQUIVALENT RATIO
entities.description: A metric used to evaluate the efficiency of intercropping systems

entities.name: RELAY INTERCROPPING
entities.description: A method where the second crop is planted after the first is already established

entities.name: BELOW-GROUND NICHE DIFFERENTIATION
entities.description: The spatial separation of root systems to reduce competition for resources

entities.name: MONOCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing a single crop species in a field
